## 🌎 Geopolitics

### Trump Lifts Strait of Hormuz Blockade as US-Iran Ceasefire Ends Six-Month War

The United States and Iran finalized a memorandum of understanding on June 14, 2026, halting the conflict that began February 28. Trump immediately lifted the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most critical chokepoint for oil shipments. The deal includes Iranian restrictions on strait transit ending, U.S. military assets withdrawing from the region, and comprehensive sanctions relief. Energy markets absorbed this within days—Brent crude slipped 6% on the news—but the supply-chain damage will persist for weeks.

**Why it matters.** This reshapes India's energy security calculus in real time. The disruption already forced fertilizer plants across India, Bangladesh, and Pakistan to shutter entirely, gutting ag supply chains that feed 1.8 billion people. Your prop research practice is watching commodity hedges unwind; the quant teams pricing fertilizer forwards are pricing a new baseline. Longer term, India's dual strategy—diversifying away from Gulf suppliers while accelerating renewables—just got pressure-tested. The Strait of Hormuz carries 21% of global oil; India imports 80% of its oil, and about 30% transits that strait. This proves the dependency is not abstract.

**Action.** Watch the monsoon ag reports in late July; fertilizer prices will signal whether the shutdown fed into food inflation or whether inventories absorbed it. Read the Atlantic Council brief on US-India energy cooperation—the void left by reduced Iran tensions is where India's Indo-Pacific energy partnerships (Vietnam, Indonesia, Mozambique) scale. If you're tracking macro hedges, basis spreads on Indian refined products are your leading indicator.

*Sources: [[https://www.britannica.com/event/2026-Iran-war](https://www.britannica.com/event/2026-Iran-war)]([https://www.britannica.com/event/2026-Iran-war](https://www.britannica.com/event/2026-Iran-war)) · [[https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/energysource/indias-energy-security-at-a-crossroads-the-hormuz-crisis-and-an-opportunity-for-us-india-cooperation/](https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/energysource/indias-energy-security-at-a-crossroads-the-hormuz-crisis-and-an-opportunity-for-us-india-cooperation/)]([https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/energysource/indias-energy-security-at-a-crossroads-the-hormuz-crisis-and-an-opportunity-for-us-india-cooperation/](https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/energysource/indias-energy-security-at-a-crossroads-the-hormuz-crisis-and-an-opportunity-for-us-india-cooperation/))*

### India and Japan Deepen AI, Defense, and Energy Ties as Indo-Pacific Competition Intensifies

Prime Minister Modi and Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi signed a slate of new agreements during the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit in New Delhi, expanding cooperation in artificial intelligence, defense systems, critical minerals, and energy security. Bilateral trade reached $27.5 billion in FY2025-26, with Japanese investment accelerating despite global uncertainty. The pact reflects both nations' effort to cement technological and strategic alignment as China and Russia deepen ties and Myanmar becomes a contested zone—Chinese and Indian delegations traded state visits last month to court the same government.

**Why it matters.** This is India's hedge strategy in motion. You're an AI engineer in Hyderabad; this deal signals that India and Japan see AI as a pillar of strategic sovereignty, not just tech talent arbitrage. The defense and critical-minerals components matter more: Japan needs rare-earth security for semiconductor production, and India is pushing to become a manufacturing hub outside China's orbit. For your consulting practice, this signals where industrial policy is moving—allied democracies carving out supply chains. India's energy diversification play also reaches Japan: renewable energy cooperation and supply-chain resilience for batteries and semiconductors are now formal commitments, not aspirations.

**Action.** Track Ministry of External Affairs announcements on the AI working group's scope—will it cover semiconductor design, LLM training, or just research collaboration? Monitor Japanese FDI into Indian tech zones for the next quarter; this deal's teeth are in capital deployment. Read McKinsey's 2026 geopolitics-and-trade update for how India's position is shifting relative to Vietnam and Bangladesh in the reshoring narrative.

*Sources: [[https://moderndiplomacy.eu/2026/07/02/india-japan-sign-ai-defence-and-energy-deals-after-modi-takaichi-summit/](https://moderndiplomacy.eu/2026/07/02/india-japan-sign-ai-defence-and-energy-deals-after-modi-takaichi-summit/)]([https://moderndiplomacy.eu/2026/07/02/india-japan-sign-ai-defence-and-energy-deals-after-modi-takaichi-summit/](https://moderndiplomacy.eu/2026/07/02/india-japan-sign-ai-defence-and-energy-deals-after-modi-takaichi-summit/)) · [[https://www.mckinsey.com/mgi/our-research/geopolitics-and-the-geometry-of-global-trade-2026-update](https://www.mckinsey.com/mgi/our-research/geopolitics-and-the-geometry-of-global-trade-2026-update/)]([https://www.mckinsey.com/mgi/our-research/geopolitics-and-the-geometry-of-global-trade-2026-update](https://www.mckinsey.com/mgi/our-research/geopolitics-and-the-geometry-of-global-trade-2026-update/))*

## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 with agentic autonomy at commodity pricing

Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5 on June 30, 2026, now the default model for Free and Pro users as of July 1. The 1M-token-context model delivers near-Opus 4.8 reasoning performance at $2/$10 per million input/output tokens through August 31 (then $3/$15 standard pricing). Sonnet 5 is Anthropic's first Sonnet capable of autonomous tool use—planning multi-step tasks, invoking terminals and browsers without intervention, and self-checking outputs without prompting.

**Why it matters.** Agentic inference at Sonnet pricing inverts your deployment calculus. Until now, autonomous workflows forced a choice between Opus (capable but expensive) and Sonnet (fast but hamstrung on complex reasoning). Sonnet 5 closes that gap—it completes tasks that Sonnet 4.6 cannot, with lower safety-violation rates than its predecessor. For engineers building autonomous systems, this is the first frontier model affordable enough to run agents continuously without metering. The 1M-token context window handles multi-turn histories without truncation, critical for agents that must maintain full decision logs.

**Action.** Benchmark Sonnet 5 head-to-head against Opus 4.8 on your agentic workflows. Run it on tool-use benchmarks (OSWorld agent performance, code generation with terminal feedback) before committing. The introductory pricing expires August 31—volume-test now while rates are favorable. If your agents are today running on Opus out of capability necessity, this model may halve inference costs.

*Sources: [[https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-5](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-5)]([https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-5](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-5)) · [[https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/anthropic-launches-claude-sonnet-5-as-a-cheaper-way-to-run-agents/](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/anthropic-launches-claude-sonnet-5-as-a-cheaper-way-to-run-agents/)]([https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/anthropic-launches-claude-sonnet-5-as-a-cheaper-way-to-run-agents/](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/anthropic-launches-claude-sonnet-5-as-a-cheaper-way-to-run-agents/)) · [[https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/30/claude-sonnet-5/](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/30/claude-sonnet-5/)]([https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/30/claude-sonnet-5/](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/30/claude-sonnet-5/))*

### AlphaEvolve evolutionary agent proves out algorithmic optimization at scale

Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve—a Gemini-powered evolutionary coding agent—has shifted from research prototype to infrastructure tool, with deployment now expanding to National Laboratories. The system uses a generational loop: Gemini Flash and Pro variants propose algorithm candidates, automated evaluators score them, and the fittest survive to next generation. AlphaEvolve has discovered a 4×4 complex matrix multiplication algorithm using 48 scalar multiplications, achieved 23% speedup in FlashAttention (the kernel training Gemini itself), recovered 0.7% compute across Google data centers, and advanced 20% of fifty open mathematical problems.

**Why it matters.** This is evidence that agentic systems can do real optimization work, not just emulation. FlashAttention is a heavily optimized, human-engineered kernel; a 23% speedup represents genuine algorithmic discovery, not token prediction. The 0.7% data-center recovery on Google's already-hyper-tuned infrastructure is material—at hyperscale, 0.7% is money. For engineers building optimization systems, this proves evolutionary loops with LLMs as generators outperform human iteration on hard problems. The expansion to National Labs signals confidence in cross-domain transfer (the system has already touched genomics, quantum circuits, chip design, and math).

**Action.** Study AlphaEvolve's evolutionary architecture if you're optimizing compute-intensive kernels or algorithms. The generational filtering approach (propose → evaluate → select) is generalizable to your domain. If you have open algorithmic problems (compression, routing, scheduling), prototyping an AlphaEvolve-style loop on Gemini or Claude agents could yield unexpectedly large gains. Read the published methodology to understand the automated-evaluator design—that's where most teams stumble.

*Sources: [[https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-impact/](https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-impact/)]([https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-impact/](https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-impact/)) · [[https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-a-gemini-powered-coding-agent-for-designing-advanced-algorithms/](https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-a-gemini-powered-coding-agent-for-designing-advanced-algorithms/)]([https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-a-gemini-powered-coding-agent-for-designing-advanced-algorithms/](https://deepmind.google/blog/alphaevolve-a-gemini-powered-coding-agent-for-designing-advanced-algorithms/)) · [[https://www.hackster.io/news/google-s-deepmind-announces-alphaevolve-an-llm-based-ai-agent-for-the-hard-problems-e0dcf3642c1f](https://www.hackster.io/news/google-s-deepmind-announces-alphaevolve-an-llm-based-ai-agent-for-the-hard-problems-e0dcf3642c1f)]([https://www.hackster.io/news/google-s-deepmind-announces-alphaevolve-an-llm-based-ai-agent-for-the-hard-problems-e0dcf3642c1f](https://www.hackster.io/news/google-s-deepmind-announces-alphaevolve-an-llm-based-ai-agent-for-the-hard-problems-e0dcf3642c1f))*

## 🇮🇳 India

### BatX Energies Raises ₹105 Crore Series A to Dominate India's Battery Recycling Push

Gurugram-based battery recycling startup BatX Energies closed a ₹105 crore Series A round on July 2, 2026, led by IvyCap Ventures with participation from Zephyr Peacock, Mankind Pharma Family Office, Excel Industries Family Office, and JITO. The startup recovers battery-grade lithium, cobalt, nickel, and graphite from end-of-life EV batteries and manufacturing scrap. BatX operates shredding and hydrometallurgy capacity of 5,000 metric tonnes annually each, with 20,000+ MTA across micro-facilities nationally, plus 20 MWh refurbishing capability.

**Why it matters.** India's EV charging infrastructure hit 72,000 stations under the PM E-Drive scheme, creating an imminent supply crunch as batteries reach end-of-life. This round signals investor conviction that India cannot depend on Chinese, Korean, or Japanese battery imports—domestic recycling closes a critical gap in the deeptech supply chain. For engineers building AI/ML solutions in EV optimization, battery management systems, or materials science, BatX's scale of recovered materials creates a testbed for localized innovation. The recovered cathode active materials are inputs for next-generation battery R&D, making this less a recycling play and more infrastructure for India's energy transition stack.

**Action.** Track BatX's upcoming cathode active material production milestones—this is where India's battery IP emerges. Review IvyCap's deeptech portfolio for pattern recognition in what capital prioritizes. If building in battery-tech or EV infra, the sourcing of domestically recycled materials becomes a technical specification within 24 months.

*Sources: [[EVreporter](https://evreporter.com/batx-energies-raises-%E2%82%B9105-crore-in-series-a-funding/)]([EVreporter](https://evreporter.com/batx-energies-raises-%E2%82%B9105-crore-in-series-a-funding/)) · [[Inc42](https://inc42.com/buzz/battery-tech-startup-batx-energies-nets-%E2%82%B9105-cr-to-bolster-rd/)]([Inc42](https://inc42.com/buzz/battery-tech-startup-batx-energies-nets-%E2%82%B9105-cr-to-bolster-rd/)) · [[Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/news/batx-energies-raises-rs-105-cr-in-series-a-round-led-by-ivycap-ventures-12125741)]([Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/news/batx-energies-raises-rs-105-cr-in-series-a-round-led-by-ivycap-ventures-12125741))*

### RBI's AI Kill-Switch Mandate Rewires Every Indian Bank's Tech Stack by 2027

On June 23–24, 2026, the Reserve Bank of India released draft guidance mandating that all banks, NBFCs, and regulated entities must deploy "kill-switch arrangements" for every AI/ML model in production, with public comment open until July 24, 2026. The framework requires board-level approval for all model lifecycles, risk-tiered explainability thresholds, and permanent third-party accountability—making India the first major economy to codify mandatory human-override architecture for deployed AI systems.

**Why it matters.** If you're engineering AI/ML for fintech, lending, or fraud detection inside an Indian financial entity, this is your technical spec now. Kill switches mean your model cannot run unsupervised; explainability thresholds dictate how much of your decision logic remains opaque; third-party accountability means your startup absorbs liability if models misfire. This shift favors interpretable models (decision trees, rule-based systems, attention mechanisms) over black-box architectures, forcing a re-architecture of teams that built on XGBoost or neural networks without auditability. The mandates also create M&A dynamics—banks will acquire smaller AI shops with proven explainability-first stacks rather than retrain in-house teams.

**Action.** Read the full RBI draft guidance on Model Risk Management when finalized (expected post-July 24). If you're pitching to Indian banks or NBFCs, your AI/ML product must include kill-switch, explainability, and audit-trail design documents. Track which startups pivot from black-box to explainable-AI architecture—they're signaling competitive advantage for post-2027 fintech deals.

*Sources: [[MediaNama](https://www.medianama.com/2026/06/223-rbi-ai-guidelines-2026-banks-kill-switch/)]([MediaNama](https://www.medianama.com/2026/06/223-rbi-ai-guidelines-2026-banks-kill-switch/)) · [[CorpLawUpdates](https://www.corplawupdates.in/updates/rbi-draft-guidance-model-risk-management-2026-ai-ml-banks-nbfcs)]([CorpLawUpdates](https://www.corplawupdates.in/updates/rbi-draft-guidance-model-risk-management-2026-ai-ml-banks-nbfcs)) · [[Outlook Business](https://www.outlookbusiness.com/economy-and-policy/why-rbi-wants-banks-to-put-tighter-checks-on-ai-use)]([Outlook Business](https://www.outlookbusiness.com/economy-and-policy/why-rbi-wants-banks-to-put-tighter-checks-on-ai-use))*

## 📈 Markets

### Weak US Jobs Crush Semiconductor Valuations, Rate-Hike Bets Off Table Through Summer

The US economy added just 57,000 nonfarm payrolls in June, roughly half the 115,000 consensus forecast, sending the unemployment rate down to 4.2% on a decline in labor force participation to 61.5% — the lowest since March 2021. This disappointment collapsed expectations for Fed action: CME FedWatch now prices zero rate hikes through July-August and removed September hikes entirely. The repricing hit semiconductors hardest, with Nasdaq-100 closing down 1.61% on July 2, dragging Applied Materials -7.4%, Micron -7%, and AMD -4.3%, while the S&P 500 edged flat at 7,482.70 and the Dow rose 1.14% to 52,900.07.

**Why it matters.** Despite the jobs miss, Fed Chair Kevin Warsh doubled down on inflation focus heading into summer — core PCE has risen to 3.3% and the Committee remains "unanimous and unambiguous" on fighting price growth. This creates a stagflationary undertone: weaker growth with persistent inflation caps rate cuts and pushes capital away from premium-valuation tech into cyclical plays and emerging-market debt. For Indian markets, the signal is dual-channel: a softer dollar on no-hike expectations weakens INR resistance (USD/INR at 95.15), while record foreign portfolio inflows into Indian sovereign bonds reached ₹55,518 crore in June — 15x normal monthly run-rate — as tax incentives and potential Bloomberg index inclusion draw $5.8 billion into G-Secs. Sensex closed up 0.75% on July 3 at 77,502, with IT stocks rallying 4-5.6% as rupee weakness helps exporters. The India 10Y G-Sec yield has compressed to 6.74%, a 15-week low, creating a carry-trade signal for quant strategies betting on yield-curve flattening in DM economies.

**Action.** Watch the July 28–29 Fed decision for forward guidance on rate-hold extension and any hawkish language on core inflation. If PCE or core PCE prints hot in coming weeks, the Fed may signal October hike optionality, which would pull FPI flows back into US Treasuries (currently 4.49% on 10Y UST) and reverse the INR strength. Monitor Nifty 50 and Bank Nifty (currently tracking 24,175 and 58,003) for a break below technical support on any India bond yield widening; a sustained drop in foreign inflows would reverse the equity rally.

*Sources: [[US Jobs Report June 2026](https://www.bls.gov/news.release/pdf/empsit.pdf)]([US Jobs Report June 2026](https://www.bls.gov/news.release/pdf/empsit.pdf)) · [[CME FedWatch Tool July 2026](https://www.cmegroup.com/tools-information/calendars/fedwatch.html)]([CME FedWatch Tool July 2026](https://www.cmegroup.com/tools-information/calendars/fedwatch.html)) · [[India FPI Bond Flows June 2026](https://www.businesstoday.in/latest/economy/story/why-fpis-poured-money-into-indias-debt-market-in-june-even-as-they-sold-equities-540277-2026-07-01)]([India FPI Bond Flows June 2026](https://www.businesstoday.in/latest/economy/story/why-fpis-poured-money-into-indias-debt-market-in-june-even-as-they-sold-equities-540277-2026-07-01)) · [[Fed Chair Warsh Inflation Remarks](https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260617a.htm)]([Fed Chair Warsh Inflation Remarks](https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260617a.htm))*

### Strait of Hormuz Stabilization Pressures Oil Below $71, Underpins Global Equity Valuations

Brent crude fell to $70.57 per barrel on July 2, extending a slide that saw oil record its steepest quarterly drop since the start of the pandemic (April–June), as indirect US-Iran talks in Doha progressed and traffic through the Strait resumed. Negotiations under the 60-day interim agreement have pushed vessel transits back to 34 daily — still a fraction of the pre-crisis 100-per-day baseline, but steady enough to ease near-term supply fears. Meanwhile, gold held above $4,100 per ounce on Friday, up 2%+ from earlier weakness, drawing support from disappointing US employment data.

**Why it matters.** The Strait stabilization removes a geopolitical risk premium that was pricing in a $90+ oil scenario in April–May 2026. This commodity reset tightens margins for oil-heavy economies (INR/USD at 95.15 supports India's import bill) and lowers the cost-push inflation component that the Fed has cited as a barrier to rate cuts. For quant prop desks, the oil-gold divergence signals a real-rate shift: gold climbing on weaker growth expectations while oil falls on supply relief creates a carry-trade opportunity via commodity-hedged equity portfolios (long India equities/short oil futures). The weaker crude also supports multiple expansion for capital-intensive sectors like tech and auto, explaining why Sensex rallied despite weak global cues.

**Action.** Track June CPI data (UK, EU, India scheduled for next week) to confirm that oil's slide is flowing through to headline prints. Any sign of geopolitical escalation (Iran threatening straits again or new US strikes) would reverse the oil weakness and spike the risk premium, closing the carry. Watch 10Y G-Sec yields (currently 6.74%) for a sustained hold below 6.80% to confirm foreign flows remain intact; a breach would signal that commodity stagflation fears are reversing the debt inflows.

*Sources: [[Brent Crude Oil Historical Prices](https://www.investing.com/commodities/brent-oil-historical-data)]([Brent Crude Oil Historical Prices](https://www.investing.com/commodities/brent-oil-historical-data)) · [[US-Iran Negotiations and Strait of Hormuz 60-Day Agreement](https://www.npr.org/2026/06/18/nx-s1-5863517/trump-iran-deal-blockade-strait-hormuz)]([US-Iran Negotiations and Strait of Hormuz 60-Day Agreement](https://www.npr.org/2026/06/18/nx-s1-5863517/trump-iran-deal-blockade-strait-hormuz)) · [[Iran July 2 Tanker Warning](https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2026/jul/2/iran-warning-oil-tankers-use-approved-routes-strait-hormuz-face/)]([Iran July 2 Tanker Warning](https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2026/jul/2/iran-warning-oil-tankers-use-approved-routes-strait-hormuz-face/)) · [[Gold Price and Macro Support](https://www.investing.com/analysis/gold-oil-prices-stay-elevated-but-bullion-looks-elsewhere-200681822)]([Gold Price and Macro Support](https://www.investing.com/analysis/gold-oil-prices-stay-elevated-but-bullion-looks-elsewhere-200681822))*

## 🇮🇳 Indian Stocks

### VISL (NSE): +9.98% as Vedanta Iron Steel hits upper circuit in 12th straight rally

Vedanta Iron Steel (VISL) locked in a 10% upper circuit at ₹42.65 on July 2, extending a relentless 12-session winning streak since its June 15 listing. The newly-demerged iron and steel entity has gained 113.25% from its ₹20 listing price, with trade volume reflecting strong institutional demand. The rally underscores explosive market appetite for Vedanta's conglomerate breakup, which seeks to unlock separate valuations for each business.

**Why it matters.** The demerger structure—where existing Vedanta shareholders received one VISL share per holding—mirrors the broader consolidation of India's steel sector ahead of potential capex cycles. Premji Invest's institutional validation (acquiring 4.84 crore shares at ₹21.02 each in a ₹102 crore block deal shortly after listing) signals confidence in integrated iron-ore-to-steel plays during an inflationary commodity environment. For domestic investors, the move reflects unlocking value when single-business valuations trade at discounts to conglomerate sums.

**Action.** Monitor the ₹45 level as technical resistance; sustained momentum above ₹43 suggests institutional accumulation continues. SEBI disclosures and BSE announcements will track large block deals. Watch Vedanta Ltd (VEDL) for correlation—any discount between parent and subsidiary valuations triggers arbitrage flows. Management commentary on capex and FY27 guidance (typically in Q1 earnings, expected July-August) will reset fair value.

*Sources: [[HDFCSky - VISL Upper Circuit Rally](https://hdfcsky.com/news/vedanta-iron-steel-share-price-upper-circuit-12th-session-stock-doubles-since-listing)]([HDFCSky - VISL Upper Circuit Rally](https://hdfcsky.com/news/vedanta-iron-steel-share-price-upper-circuit-12th-session-stock-doubles-since-listing)) · [[TickerTape - VISL Live Price](https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/vedanta-iron-and-steel-VISL)]([TickerTape - VISL Live Price](https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/vedanta-iron-and-steel-VISL)) · [[Yahoo Finance - VISL.NS](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/VISL.NS/)]([Yahoo Finance - VISL.NS](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/VISL.NS/))*

### HDFCBANK (NSE): +1.4% as Rajiv Kumar named part-time chairman, strengthening governance credibility

HDFC Bank traded at ₹795.9 on July 2, posting a +1.4% intraday gain within a ₹794–₹805.45 range after the board appointed Rajiv Kumar—former Finance Secretary and ex-IAS officer—as additional independent director and part-time chairman-designate pending RBI approval. Kumar's appointment, effective June 30, brings three decades of banking reform expertise, including NPA cleanup and PSU consolidation, to India's largest private bank by assets.

**Why it matters.** HDFC Bank's new leadership structure signals a governance refresh after the bank's 20% decline over the past year (compared to Nifty 50's 6.2% fall). Kumar's reputation in systemic financial restructuring—he led the merger of Associate Banks into SBI and recapitalization of public-sector banks—provides credibility to address efficiency concerns in deposit-cost management and credit growth. For Nifty 50 tracking portfolios, this governance signal ripples across banking sentiment: if institutional confidence returns to HDFC Bank, sector rotation away from deposit-intensive PSUs may reverse.

**Action.** File the RBI approval letter once published in exchange disclosures (expected within 2 weeks). Jefferies' ₹1,050 target (31% upside from current) reflects market's repricing on leadership transition. Next catalyst: Q1 FY27 earnings (expected mid-July); management commentary on margin compression, credit-deposit gap, and deposit pricing will determine whether the governance refresh translates to operational turnaround.

*Sources: [[TipRanks - Rajiv Kumar Appointment](https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/hdfc-bank-names-rajiv-kumar-independent-director-and-part-time-chairman-designate)]([TipRanks - Rajiv Kumar Appointment](https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/hdfc-bank-names-rajiv-kumar-independent-director-and-part-time-chairman-designate)) · [[BusinessToday - HDFC Shares After Rajiv Kumar Appointment](https://www.businesstoday.in/markets/stocks/story/hdfc-bank-shares-in-focus-as-rajiv-kumar-named-part-time-chairman-539861-2026-06-30)]([BusinessToday - HDFC Shares After Rajiv Kumar Appointment](https://www.businesstoday.in/markets/stocks/story/hdfc-bank-shares-in-focus-as-rajiv-kumar-named-part-time-chairman-539861-2026-06-30)) · [[Kotak - HDFC Bank Live Price](https://www.kotakneo.com/stocks/hdfc-bank-share-price/)]([Kotak - HDFC Bank Live Price](https://www.kotakneo.com/stocks/hdfc-bank-share-price/))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### SNDK (NASDAQ): -11.0% on profit-taking amid memory chip supply glut fears

SanDisk plunged 11.0% to close July 2 at $1,099.17, erasing a $130.42 move after surging 720% in the first half of 2026. Micron, Applied Materials, and Lam Research each fell 10.0%, while the PHLX Semiconductor Index (SOX) dropped 6.7% after doubling in Q2. Profit-taking dominated: memory chip euphoria peaked mid-quarter and reversed hard as investors rotated from hardware into software, while concerns about a memory supply glut haunted valuations trading at stratospheric multiples.

**Why it matters.** The memory complex led the broader semiconductor rally through Q2—SanDisk and Micron each outran the S&P 500 by 600%+—so a retracement here sets the tone for AI-driven capex decisions across the cloud stack. Applied Materials and Lam Research supply the tooling for fabs that build these chips; their pullback signals margin anxiety among equipment buyers. For India's chip and semiconductor ecosystem, this serves as a caution against euphoria-driven valuations and a reminder that even record earnings (Micron posted a $25.11 EPS beat on June 24 vs $20.20 consensus) cannot sustain parabolic moves without a fundamental reset.

**Action.** Check SanDisk's Q3 2026 earnings deck (filed post-market, typically via IR portal) for guidance on demand and wafer starts into Q4. Monitor Applied Materials' June quarter data on spot margins and equipment orders from TSMC, Samsung, and Intel fabs. The $1,020 level is technical support for SNDK; a break below signals capitulation.

### META (NASDAQ): -4.9% as cloud compute gambit triggers capex rout

Meta Platforms closed July 2 at $582.89, down $30.01 or 4.9% after jumping 8.8% on July 1 to $612.91 when Bloomberg broke that the company is building Meta Compute, a cloud service to rent surplus GPU and custom silicon capacity. Volume of 45.5 million shares was triple the daily average, signaling reversal on the second day. The reversal reflected a calculus shift: initial euphoria over Meta's $200B+ AI capex paying off via monetization gave way to concerns that massive ongoing capex commitments would delay margin expansion and compete directly with CoreWeave and Nebius, which tumbled 14.0% and 17.0% respectively on fears of Meta undercutting cloud GPU rates by 20-30%.

**Why it matters.** Meta's cloud compute play signals hyperscaler competitive dynamics are shifting from consuming all chips to becoming chip resellers—a margin compression risk for the entire GPU rental market and a capitalization risk for the capex thesis itself. The reversal also reflects the June jobs report (57,000 payrolls vs 100k+ forecast), which dampened risk appetite into the long holiday weekend. For Indian IT services and AI infrastructure vendors, the message is clear: hyperscalers are internalizing AI workloads and cloud margin will compress before it expands.

**Action.** Read Meta's July 1 announcement (via investor.atmeta.com/newsroom) for launch timeline, MTIA/H100 ratio on offer, and geographic rollout. Monitor AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud pricing announcements for competitive responses. The $590–$600 level is now a short-term pivot for META; a break above $600 reinstates the uptrend.
